initiatives.name: Scuttle Chicago
initiatives.description: A grassroots collective coordinating mutual aid and community support initiatives in Chicago. They organize direct assistance, resource sharing, and solidarity networks while building community power through collective action and neighborhood organizing.

initiatives.name: Avondale Mutual Aid
initiatives.description: All-volunteer group- Avondale, Chicago.

initiatives.name: Edgewater Mutual Aid
initiatives.description: EMAN: Abolitionist Mutual Aid Network in Edgewater, Chicago

initiatives.name: Evanston Community Fridges
initiatives.description: A food based mutual aid project led by the community for the community!

initiatives.name: Mutual Aid Dawning
initiatives.description: A mutual aid collective building liberation culture and projects in the suburbs of shikaakwa (settler named Chicago, IL).

initiatives.name: Logan Square Mutual Aid
initiatives.description: Logan Square Mutual Aid is here to make helping one another easy. In the face of widespread inequity and an ongoing pandemic, the most effective thing we can do is to be there for each other.

initiatives.name: Pilsen Solidarity Network
initiatives.description: Neighbor to Neighbor Mutual Aid

initiatives.name: Westside Mutual Aid
initiatives.description: Mutual aid network between Austin, East Garfield Park, North Lawndale, West Garfield Park, and West Loop.

initiatives.name: Albany Park Mutual Aid
initiatives.description: We support neighbors in need by providing goods such as groceries, household supplies, diapers, and the like. We also provide advice and resources according to specific requests.

initiatives.name: HP Solidarity Network
initiatives.description: Neighbors helping neighbors in Humboldt Park.

initiatives.name: West Town Mutual Aid
initiatives.description: West Town Mutual Aid is an all-volunteer, grassroots group operating in Chicago in Ukrainian Village, Bucktown, Wicker Park, and the surrounding areas.

initiatives.name: Irving Park Mutual Aid
initiatives.description: Working to provide support & relief to our neighbors in Irving Park impacted by the COVID-19 crisis.

initiatives.name: Elgin in Solidarity with Black Lives Matter
initiatives.description: Organize, educate, agitate, liberate! This is a digital community space to share resources & knowledge.

initiatives.name: Mayfair Mutual Aid
initiatives.description: Neighbors help with grocery and medicine pick up/delivery, provide cash assistance for rent and other bills, and share resources for medical care, employment, and other help.

initiatives.name: Food Not Bombs Chicago
initiatives.description: Sharing free vegan and vegetarian meals with anyone who is hungry.

initiatives.name: Rogers Park Food Not Bombs
initiatives.description: A grassroots collective fostering mutual aid through free food distribution and community care in Rogers Park. They coordinate weekly meal shares, food recovery initiatives, and solidarity networks while building power through food justice organizing and creating spaces for collective nourishment.

initiatives.name: Global Gardens Chicago
initiatives.description: A vacant lot transformed into a vibrant farm in which 75+ refugees grow vegetables in Chicago's Albany Park neighborhood.

initiatives.name: Food Not Bombs Humboldt Park
initiatives.description: Sharing groceries in Chicago's Humboldt Park neighborhood.

initiatives.name: Veggie Mijas Chicago
initiatives.description: A collective focusing on food justice, sustainability & plant-based lifestyles.

initiatives.name: Hammond Community Garden
initiatives.description: A food justice/sovereignty project which seeks to bring ​neighbors and local community members together in a ​collaborative setting, engage in mutual aid, and ​collectively meet our need for fresh, healthy food to be ​freely available and shared.

initiatives.name: Chicagoland Food Sovereignty Coalition
initiatives.description: CFSC is a coalition of Chicago and Cook County mutual aid groups working together to create #foodsovereignty for all people in all of our communities.

initiatives.name: The Love Fridge
initiatives.description: The Love Fridge is a mutual aid group working to ensure every Chicagoan has access to fresh food by maintaining free community fridges in neighborhoods across the city.

initiatives.name: Chicagoland Food Sovereignty Coalition
initiatives.description: CFSC is a coalition of autonomous mutual aid groups working to reimagine a resilient, sustainable and equitable local food system based on food sovereignty.

initiatives.name: Rogers Park Free Store
initiatives.description: Mutual Aid space for the Rogers Park neighborhood. Open hours are on Saturday from 12-2pm

initiatives.name: Chicago Coalition to End Homelessness
initiatives.description: A statewide organization fostering mutual aid and community power through housing justice and homelessness prevention across Illinois. They coordinate legal aid, direct support, and solidarity networks while building power through policy advocacy, community organizing, and creating pathways for housing access and stability.

initiatives.name: Chicago Warming Centers
initiatives.description: Network of warming centers and emergency shelters providing support during extreme weather.

initiatives.name: Eastlake Tenants Union
initiatives.description: Organizing to improve the quality of life in East Lake managed buildings across Chicago

initiatives.name: Tenants United Chicago
initiatives.description: Our mission is to build neighborhood justice and grassroots democracy by developing autonomous tenants unions that are welcoming for all.

initiatives.name: Autonomous Yurt Union Chicago
initiatives.description: autonomous yurt-inspired design builds & deploys in support of unhoused neighbors in Chicago

initiatives.name: Unraveled Press
initiatives.description: A radical publishing collective coordinating mutual aid and community support initiatives through accessible literature and zine distribution in Chicago. They organize resource sharing, political education, and solidarity networks while building community power through radical publishing and collective organizing.

initiatives.name: Kenwood Oakland Community Organization
initiatives.description: By engaging low-income and working families, KOCO develops leaders who impact public policy and work hard to improve life in local communities.

initiatives.name: Little Village Environmental Justice Organization (LVEJO)
initiatives.description: A grassroots organization building power through community organizing, mutual aid, and advocacy in Chicago's Little Village neighborhood. LVEJO works on environmental justice, public transit access, food justice, and community development while centering the leadership of working-class residents.

initiatives.name: The TRiiBE
initiatives.description: A digital media platform fostering mutual aid and community power through narrative journalism and cultural documentation in Chicago. They coordinate storytelling initiatives, resource sharing, and solidarity networks while amplifying Black voices and building power through grassroots media, arts coverage, and creating spaces for community-driven journalism.

initiatives.name: PO Box Collective
initiatives.description: A grassroots community space fostering mutual aid and radical education through art, workshops, and collective organizing in Rogers Park. They coordinate skill-sharing, resource distribution, and solidarity networks while building power through community-led programming, political education, and creating accessible spaces for movement building.

initiatives.name: CAARP (Chicago Alliance Against Racist and Political Repression)
initiatives.description: A grassroots organization fostering mutual aid and community power through anti-racist organizing and political advocacy in Chicago. They coordinate direct support, community defense, and solidarity networks while building power through collective action against racist and political repression, community organizing, and creating spaces for racial justice and liberation.

initiatives.name: Let Us Breathe Collective
initiatives.description: A Black-led organization fostering mutual aid and community power through cultural organizing and abolitionist movement building in Chicago. They coordinate direct support, political education, and solidarity networks while building power through arts activism, transformative justice, and creating spaces for Black liberation and community care.

initiatives.name: Southside Together
initiatives.description: A grassroots collective fostering mutual aid and community power through neighborhood organizing and solidarity work on Chicago's South Side. They coordinate direct support, resource sharing, and community defense initiatives while building power through collective action, political education, and creating spaces for neighborhood-based organizing and mutual support.

initiatives.name: Dare to Struggle Chicago
initiatives.description: A revolutionary organization fostering mutual aid and community solidarity through collective organizing in Chicago. They coordinate political education, direct action support, and solidarity networks while building power through community organizing and creating spaces for collective study and struggle.

initiatives.name: Southwest Collective
initiatives.description: Breaking down barriers & building up communities on Chicago's southwest side.

initiatives.name: Brave Space Alliance
initiatives.description: A Black-led, trans-led LGBTQ+ center fostering mutual aid and community power on the South Side of Chicago. They coordinate direct support services, resource distribution, and solidarity networks while building power through community organizing, trans mutual aid programs, and creating brave spaces for Black and Brown LGBTQ+ people.

initiatives.name: Support Ho(s)e
initiatives.description: A collective of sex workers and allies providing mutual aid, harm reduction, and community support.

initiatives.name: Chicago Abortion Fund
initiatives.description: A mutual aid organization providing financial, logistical, and emotional support to people seeking abortion care in Chicago and Illinois. They work to make abortion accessible to everyone through direct funding assistance and community support networks.

initiatives.name: AIDS Foundation Chicago
initiatives.description: A grassroots organization fostering mutual aid and community power through HIV/AIDS support and health justice in Chicago. They coordinate direct services, resource sharing, and solidarity networks while building power through community health initiatives, advocacy, and creating pathways for equitable healthcare access and support.

initiatives.name: Chicago Action Medical
initiatives.description: A collective of street medics fostering mutual aid through direct action medical support and health justice organizing. They coordinate emergency care at protests, medical training workshops, and solidarity networks while building power through community health education, harm reduction, and creating spaces for grassroots healthcare.

initiatives.name: Chi Care
initiatives.description: Help us provide warm meals, clean water, basic necessities, and a restored sense of dignity to individuals experiencing homelessness in Chicagoland.

initiatives.name: Sex Workers Outreach Project Chicago
initiatives.description: Sex Workers Outreach Project Chicago is a Black-led, grassroots organization dedicated to providing direct aid and advocacy for anyone working in the sex industry.

initiatives.name: Believers Bail Out
initiatives.description: We are a community led bail fund freeing Muslims from pre-trial & immigration incarceration.

initiatives.name: Chicago Community Jail Support
initiatives.description: A mutual aid collective providing direct support and resources to people being released from Cook County Jail. They coordinate daily jail support, resource distribution, and solidarity networks while building power through community care, direct action, and creating spaces for support during and after incarceration.

initiatives.name: Pushing Envelopes
initiatives.description: A grassroots organization fostering mutual aid and solidarity through letter writing and support for incarcerated LGBTQ+ people. They coordinate pen pal programs, resource sharing, and solidarity networks while building power through prisoner advocacy, community education, and creating spaces for connection across prison walls.

initiatives.name: Midwest Books to Prisoners
initiatives.description: A volunteer-run organization fostering mutual aid through free book distribution to incarcerated people since 2004. They coordinate book donations, letter writing, and solidarity networks while building power through educational access, resource sharing, and creating spaces for connection and learning across prison walls.

initiatives.name: Chicago Torture Justice Center
initiatives.description: A Center for survivors of police torture in Chicago. We are part of & support the movement to end all forms of police violence

initiatives.name: Chi Nations Youth Council
initiatives.description: Our mission is to create safe space for Native Youth through arts, activism, and education.

initiatives.name: Refugee Community Connection
initiatives.description: RCC is an all-volunteer Chicago based mutual aid organization that seeks to ease the transition of our newly arrived neighbors (refugees, asylees, and others forced to flee their home country) into permanent housing in the Chicago area

initiatives.name: Nuevos Vecinos Free Store
initiatives.description: Shopping at the Nuevos Vecinos Tienda Gratis (New Neighbors Free Store) is often the first opportunity for new arrivals to replace the clothes and shoes that have served them for more than 4,000 miles.

initiatives.name: Casa Al-Fatiha
initiatives.description: Casa Al-Fatiha is an autonomous, Chicago-based, sanctuary house for LGBTQ+ asylum seekers, people released from detention, and peer supporters.
